steps.title: Identify the modifier and what it must attach to
steps.content: The sentence opens with a long modifying phrase ending with "since uncovering fragments of a 2,000-year-old reindeer training harness in northern Siberia." Whatever noun phrase immediately follows this modifier must be the logical subject of that action — the person who did the uncovering. Only a human researcher can uncover artifacts.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank must place the correct noun right after the opening modifier so the modifier logically applies to it, and the resulting sentence must be grammatically complete.
  • Choice A — 'researcher Robert Losey has argued that domestication': places "researcher Robert Losey" immediately after the modifier, correctly identifying Losey as the one who uncovered the harness, and uses standard reported-speech structure to convey his argument. ✓
  • Choice B — 'researcher Robert Losey's argument is that domestication': places "researcher Robert Losey's argument" after the modifier, illogically implying the argument did the uncovering in Siberia. ✗
  • Choice C — 'domestication, researcher Robert Losey has argued,': places "domestication" immediately after the modifier, illogically implying that domestication itself uncovered the harness. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the argument researcher Robert Losey has made is that domestication': places "the argument" after the modifier, illogically implying the argument did the uncovering in Siberia. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
